behaviors.description: Reviewer dispatch sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack to each reviewer role.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: dispatcher -> reviewer client invocations for three roles. A unit test of one pure function can miss wiring/mapping mistakes across modules. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real network/OS/subprocess. Rejected Agentic: behavior is payload routing/config correctness, not model judgment (plumbing-only scope).
behaviors.test_description: Run dispatcher with in-process test doubles for the three reviewer clients; assert each receives the identical document bytes/id and the correct role-specific instruction pack, with no cross-role mix-ups.

behaviors.description: The live clause extractor identifies key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model extraction quality from contract text; deterministic stubs would hide missed/incorrect term extraction. Rejected Workflow: this behavior is one reviewer’s decision, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the real clause extractor on curated legal contracts with gold annotations for parties/dates/obligations/penalties; score recall/precision thresholds and fail on missed key terms.

behaviors.description: Risk-policy configuration is passed correctly to the risk assessor invocation.
behaviors.justification: This is configuration propagation across local modules (orchestrator -> risk assessor adapter). Main risk is wrong field mapping/default override at module boundary. Rejected Agentic: behavior explicitly checks parameter passing, not policy interpretation quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute orchestration with a known risk-policy config and a spy/stub risk-assessor adapter; assert the outgoing invocation contains the exact policy version, thresholds, and flags expected.

behaviors.description: The live risk assessor flags language that is problematic under policy, including indirect or euphemistic phrasing.
behaviors.justification: Behavior evaluates semantic judgment quality of a real model (especially indirect/euphemistic language). Mocked outputs would always pass and cannot reveal model misses.
behaviors.test_description: Call the live risk assessor on a benchmark set containing explicit and euphemistic risky clauses with expected policy labels; assert detection rates/false-negative bounds.

behaviors.description: Jurisdiction and regulatory profile are passed correctly to the compliance checker invocation.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at module interface: orchestration must hand off correct jurisdiction/profile payload to compliance checker. Rejected Agentic: text is strictly about passing config correctly, not compliance decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run compliance-checker invocation path with controlled jurisdiction/regulatory profile inputs and inspect the adapter call payload for exact propagation and schema correctness.

behaviors.description: The live compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all configured regulatory requirements.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real AI/ML compliance reasoning against regulations; deterministic fixtures cannot validate true decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use the live compliance checker on contracts with expert-labeled pass/fail outcomes for configured regulations; assert decision accuracy and critical-requirement coverage.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments (for example extractor says `standard` but risk assessor says `high risk`), the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is often schema/identity handoff between reviewer outputs and merger (same-clause matching + attribution), which is a cross-module interaction. Rejected Agentic: behavior judges merge preservation logic, not model output quality. Rejected System: no real OS/network/subprocess needed.
behaviors.test_description: Feed merger through the real local integration path using two reviewer-output fixtures from their normal adapters (with conflicting assessments on same clause id/span); assert unified report keeps both entries and source attribution.

behaviors.description: End-to-end orchestration (dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report) succeeds on a real contract without losing any reviewer findings.
behaviors.justification: This is a full pipeline with multiple agentic steps (three live reviewers) plus aggregation. Failures can emerge from sequencing/concurrency/handoff across multiple model decisions, which exceeds single-step Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Run full production-like pipeline on real contracts: dispatch live clause/risk/compliance reviewers in parallel, collect all outputs, merge report, and verify each reviewer finding is present (no dropped findings, correct attribution).
